analysis.summary: NASA's Artemis II mission, carrying four astronauts — Reid Wiseman, Christina Koch, Victor Glover, and Canadian Jeremy Hansen — launched on a 10-day journey around the moon, marking the first crewed lunar mission since Apollo 17 in 1972. The launch took place from Kennedy Space Center in Florida on a Wednesday, following years of preparation, safety testing, and a prior delay from February due to weather. The mission drew widespread public excitement, political attention from President Trump, and media coverage ranging from crew preparations to viewing logistics.
